title: The Relationship Between Sample Statistics and Standard Error
description: A visual explanation of how standard error measures the variability of a sample statistic (like the mean) across multiple samples from the same population.
elements: Population (True Mean μ, Standard Deviation σ)
relationships: Multiple samples are drawn from the population, each yielding a sample mean
The spread (standard deviation) of these sample means in the sampling distribution is the Standard Error
The Standard Error quantifies the precision of the sample mean as an estimate of the population mean
